hero_037.earlyGame: In the early game, the basic playstyle is to accumulate "Shadow Power" by casting skills and use enhanced Basic Attacks to dash behind the enemy and deal damage. While silencing enemies in an area and reducing magic damage taken with Skill 2, farm and harass safely.
hero_037.midGame: Use Skill 1, which manipulates a spirit, to move at high speed and actively gank other lanes. Take advantage of the Passive, which grants vision upon casting skills, to track enemy positions and launch surprise attacks. Since you can recover HP even when taking magic damage, you have an advantage against mages.
hero_037.lateGame: In the late game, take advantage of the expanded range of enhanced Basic Attacks to exploit openings in teamfights and target the enemy's carries. Use the long-range dash of Skill 3 to quickly close the distance, keeping hit-and-run tactics in mind. Since Skill 3's cooldown is significantly reduced if interrupted, it can also be used as a feint.
hero_037.teamfight: In teamfights, absorb enemy magic damage while disrupting enemy skill combos with the AoE silence of Skill 2. A playstyle utilizing high mobility is crucial—such as diving deep into the enemy formation with Skill 3 and disengaging with Skill 1, or conversely, approaching with Skill 1 and disengaging with Skill 3.
hero_037.commonMistakes: If you engage without managing your Shadow Power, you won't be able to unleash enhanced Basic Attacks, drastically reducing your burst damage. Additionally, since you are defenseless while charging Skill 3, it's dangerous to cast it in a position where you are vulnerable to enemy CC. Also note that your recovery effect is halved against physical damage.
hero_037.strengths: Overwhelming mobility through high-speed movement and long-range dashes when using skills, along with enhanced Basic Attacks that dash behind enemies, make this hero powerful. Furthermore, with the silence effect from Skill 2 and percentage HP recovery when taking magic damage, the anti-mage performance is extremely high, excelling in surprise attacks.
hero_037.weaknesses: Since resistance to physical damage is low and the recovery effect is halved, you will struggle against heroes that rely on physical attacks. Moreover, because most of your damage resource relies on enhanced Basic Attacks that consume Shadow Power, poor energy management is a critical weakness that can leave you completely helpless.

hero_038.earlyGame: In the early game, land Basic Attacks to accumulate stacks of "Discipline". Use the enhanced state at 5 stacks (increased Movement Speed and Attack Speed, plus True Damage) to gain the upper hand in damage trades. It's effective to pull enemies in with Skill 1, preventing their escape while stacking your attacks.
hero_038.midGame: Utilize the Movement Speed increase and 45% damage reduction from Skill 2 to forcibly dominate small skirmishes and 1v1 fights. Because you can reflect damage and apply a slow when taking Basic Attacks from enemies, you can play aggressively against physical attackers. Maintaining the enhanced state is crucial.
hero_038.lateGame: In the late game, aim for powerful lockdown with the "Bright Lantern" from Skill 3. By binding the main enemy damage dealer for 5 seconds so they cannot escape, and reducing their damage dealt by 20% during that time, you create an advantageous situation for your team. Maintain the frontline using your damage reduction.
hero_038.teamfight: Your primary role is to reliably catch and lock down key enemy targets with Skill 3. Activate the damage reduction and damage reflection of Skill 2 at the right timing to endure the enemy's concentrated fire. If you enter the Passive's enhanced state, your skill cooldowns are also reduced, allowing you to fight tenaciously.
hero_038.commonMistakes: If you engage in combat without Discipline stacks accumulated, you will lack both damage and durability. Also, if the Skill 3 dash misses, you lose your biggest strength—the lockdown—increasing the risk of being isolated in the enemy formation. Mistiming Skill 2 will result in being easily defeated.
hero_038.strengths: With high damage reduction and Basic Attack reflection from Skill 2, along with True Damage from the Passive, this hero boasts unparalleled strength in 1v1 brawls. The powerful lockdown ability of Skill 3, which binds an enemy to a specific location for 5 seconds while reducing their damage, is the greatest weapon.
hero_038.weaknesses: Because the true potential cannot be unleashed without continuously landing Basic Attacks at close range, this hero is weak against long-range kiting and powerful CC that prevents approaching. Additionally, the strict range and hitbox of Skill 3 make it difficult to lock down highly mobile enemies.

hero_039.earlyGame: In the early game, keeping moving to maintain the Passive "Charge" state is an absolute requirement. Seize control of the lane by utilizing the powerful knockback, max HP percentage damage, and high base damage of your Charge state Basic Attacks and Skill 1. Continuous movement and precise control are constantly required.
hero_039.midGame: Take advantage of your high mobility to quickly roam to other lanes. Ganking by knocking enemies back toward your allies with Charge state skills is highly effective. Even if you get hit by CC, you can immediately cleanse it with Skill 2, increase your Movement Speed, and re-enter the Charge state, so keep roaming the map without stopping.
hero_039.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, cast Skill 3 to halve the movement distance required to enter the Charge state, allowing you to launch continuous knockback attacks. Disrupt the enemy formation with your cavalry charge, deal max HP percentage damage, and quickly kill the defenseless enemy carries alongside your teammates.
hero_039.teamfight: Your main role is to disrupt enemy focus and destroy their formation with continuous AoE knockbacks from Skill 3. It's effective to flank behind the enemies and hit them with charge attacks to push them toward your team's territory. Conserve the CC cleanse of Skill 2, and rampage while neutralizing enemy disruption.
hero_039.commonMistakes: If you stop moving or your Movement Speed drops, breaking the Charge state, your skill performance drops drastically and you become a mere target. If you are hit by heavy lockdown immediately after wasting your CC cleanse skill, you often get killed without returning to the Charge state or fulfilling your intended role.
hero_039.strengths: The overwhelming mobility from the Passive and the powerful knockback along with max HP percentage damage in the Charge state are the biggest strengths. Possessing a self-CC cleanse skill, and the ability to continuously knock enemies back when Skill 3 is active, grants extremely high formation disruption and surprise attack capabilities.
hero_039.weaknesses: Because you must constantly keep moving, the mechanical difficulty is very high. You are highly vulnerable to powerful slow effects that cancel the Charge state or chain CC. A clear weakness is that once stopped, there is a high risk of being burst down by concentrated fire before you can re-enter the Charge state.

hero_040.earlyGame: In the early game, focus on hitting enemies with the outgoing and returning lotus of Skill 1 to apply "Floral Mark" stacks. Use the True Damage, HP recovery, and slow effect from the 4-stack explosion to gain an advantage in the laning phase. Avoid risky close combat and harass from a safe distance.
hero_040.midGame: A playstyle that dodges attacks while dealing damage with the blink and flower orbs of Skill 2 becomes crucial. If the flower orbs hit, Skill 2's cooldown is reduced, allowing you to continuously deal damage tenaciously in small skirmishes when combined with the mark explosions (recovery and True Damage).
hero_040.lateGame: In the late game, deploy the ultimate formation with Skill 3, and fighting within it grants an overwhelming skill rotation rate. Because you can continuously cast Skill 1 and 2 without pause inside the formation, the AoE True Damage and continuous recovery from mark explosions allow you to survive and fight against multiple enemies without falling.
hero_040.teamfight: Deploy the formation with Skill 3, and chain mark explosions while dodging enemy skills by fully utilizing the invulnerability frames (blink) of Skill 2 within the formation. By spreading True Damage and slows to all surrounding enemies while continuously recovering your own HP, you serve as both a damage source and an aggro magnet in teamfights.
hero_040.commonMistakes: If you fail to hit enemies with the flower orbs of Skill 2, the cooldown will not be reduced, breaking your mobility and damage output and exposing a fatal opening. Also, stepping outside the Skill 3 formation prevents your skill rotation from speeding up, losing your core strength. Getting hit by heavy CC and having your combo interrupted is also strictly forbidden.
hero_040.strengths: The loop of True Damage, HP recovery, and slow from the Passive mark explosions is extremely powerful. With the evasion capabilities from Skill 2's blink and the overwhelming skill rotation rate inside Skill 3's formation, you have the potential to annihilate multiple enemies while dodging all their attacks if your mechanical precision is high.
hero_040.weaknesses: Since you need to continuously cycle your skill combos, getting caught by hard CC like silence or stuns will leave you helpless and easily killed. Also, despite having True Damage, your base durability is low, making you highly vulnerable to sudden high burst damage that takes you down before your recovery can keep up.

hero_041.earlyGame: In the early game, apply marks to enemies from a safe distance using Skill 1 to farm and harass. Since your 3rd Basic Attack can also apply a mark, prepping it by hitting minions is also important. Play cautiously until you reach level 4 and unlock Skill 3, as your mobility is low before then.
hero_041.midGame: Utilize the mechanic where hitting a marked enemy resets the cooldown of Skill 3 to dash freely across the battlefield. Confuse the enemy with a combo where you apply marks with Skill 1, Skill 2, and enhanced Basic Attacks, and then use Skill 3 to deal damage while moving.
hero_041.lateGame: Even in late-game teamfights, managing marks and resetting Skill 3 is your lifeline. Dive into the enemy formation, disrupt their positioning, and protect yourself with the pull, stun, and shield from Skill 2. High mechanical precision and situational awareness are required to avoid hitting unmarked enemies with Skill 3.
hero_041.teamfight: Mark multiple enemies with Skill 1, then dive into the enemy formation with Skill 3. Use Skill 2 to pull enemies together, stun them, and gain a shield. Then apply marks again to either disengage or pursue with Skill 3. By continuously dealing magic damage while constantly moving, you inflict casualties while avoiding enemy attacks.
hero_041.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is hitting an unmarked enemy or an enemy whose mark just expired with Skill 3, putting it on cooldown. This completely strips you of your mobility and damage source, leaving you defenseless right in the middle of the enemy formation to be easily killed.
hero_041.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming mobility that theoretically allows infinite movement and attacks by constantly resetting Skill 3's cooldown by consuming marks. The pull, stun, and shield from Skill 2 are also excellent, giving this magic damage-based assassin extremely high carry potential.
hero_041.weaknesses: Because you become completely helpless if Skill 3 goes on cooldown, the mechanical difficulty is extremely high and heavily dependent on player skill. It is also highly vulnerable to targeted CC or silence, making you fragile if your combo is interrupted. A high reliance on mana regeneration is another weakness.

hero_042.earlyGame: In the early game, use the continuous magic damage and the movement speed/defense reduction debuff of Skill 1 to poke enemies from a safe position. Gain EXP orbs from your Passive to quickly level up. Utilize your own movement speed boost to maintain a favorable distance while preventing enemy approaches.
hero_042.midGame: Disrupt enemies with the combo of Skill 1 and Skill 2. The delayed explosion and knockup from the magic circle of Skill 2 are effective when placed to cut off the enemy's retreat. Fire the shockwave of Skill 3 from a distance to pressure enemies by lowering their HP before teamfights or while sieging towers.
hero_042.lateGame: Once you reach Lv. 15 and unlock "Divine Power Grant", use it on a key ally to uncap their level limit, overwhelming enemies with stat differences. You will also have a chance to enter "Ascension Mode", allowing you to fire the massive charging AoE attack of Skill 3 while moving, demonstrating tremendous wave clear and teamfight power.
hero_042.teamfight: From the backline, lower enemy defenses with Skill 1 and aim for knockups with Skill 2 to support your allies. When the fight stalls, fully charge Skill 3 from a safe zone to sweep the enemy formation. If Ascension Mode triggers, you can cast Skill 3 while moving, allowing you to deal damage while dodging enemy focus.
hero_042.commonMistakes: Due to your low durability and mobility, stepping too far forward will result in easily getting killed if an assassin gets close. Also, since you are defenseless while charging Skill 3, poor timing or positioning will not only result in missing the skill but also turn you into an easy target for the enemy.
hero_042.strengths: The biggest strength is the unique Passive that uncaps the level limit to Lv. 25, significantly buffing a single ally. Furthermore, the defense and movement speed reduction debuffs of Skill 1, along with the ultra-long-range, high-damage AoE attack of Skill 3, provide extremely high poking, tower defense, and sieging capabilities.
hero_042.weaknesses: Lacking hard CC or blink skills, your self-defense capabilities when enemies get close are extremely low. Also, the Passive's Divine Power grant cannot be activated until the late game, and Skill 3 takes time to charge, making it a weakness that highly mobile enemy heroes can easily dodge your attacks.

hero_043.earlyGame: In the early game, accumulate Passive marks and use your ranged enhanced Basic Attacks for harass and wave clear. Activate Skill 1's shield to prevent incoming damage, and manually detonate it before it is destroyed to deal magic damage to surrounding enemies, keeping damage trades in your favor.
hero_043.midGame: Join fights with a combo combining the charge dash of Skill 2 to stun enemies and the shield explosion of Skill 1. Always keep an eye on the minimap. When allies in other lanes are in danger, immediately use Skill 3 to teleport and grant them a shield, demanding a global playstyle capable of overturning numerical disadvantages.
hero_043.lateGame: In the late game, split push alone to draw the enemy's attention. When a teamfight is about to break out, use Skill 3 to grant a shield to your allied carry and group up. Waiting until the very end of the channeling time maximizes the shield amount and post-teleport damage.
hero_043.teamfight: Appear on the frontline while protecting a key allied character with Skill 3, simultaneously healing your own HP and surrounding allies upon arrival. Afterward, act as a tank, absorbing damage with Skill 1's shield while disrupting the enemy carry with Skill 2's dash stun, setting up an environment where your allies can deal damage.
hero_043.commonMistakes: If you receive hard CC during Skill 3's channeling, the teleport will be interrupted, leaving you isolated without being able to save your ally. Also, if the Skill 1 shield is broken by enemy attacks, the explosion damage is halved, so being too greedy and delaying the manual detonation too much is heavily discouraged.
hero_043.strengths: The greatest strength is the global support ability to rush to allies anywhere on the map with Skill 3, providing a powerful shield and healing. Also, since the shield amount and damage scale based on max HP, building as a tank allows you to balance both damage and durability, which is excellent.
hero_043.weaknesses: Heavily reliant on the Skill 3 teleport; if the channeling is interrupted, you cannot utilize your strength at all. Additionally, as a predominantly melee hero, missing the Skill 2 dash or being kited by enemies makes it difficult to deal damage. Lacking sustained damage, your solo kill potential is not high.

hero_044.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the double jump of Skill 1 and the dash of Skill 2 to quickly clear the jungle. With the Passive increasing attack speed on the 4th Basic Attack or upon skill hits, you boast high farming speed. Take advantage of your mobility to actively invade the enemy jungle and steal their resources.
hero_044.midGame: Armed with overwhelming mobility, dash across the map to gank and secure objectives. Dash in the opposite direction of your target with Skill 2, then launch a surprise attack on the enemy with the enhanced sweeping Basic Attack and the knockup from Skill 1. A playstyle focused on hit-and-run tactics to exploit enemy openings is crucial.
hero_044.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, your low durability becomes apparent, so engaging from the front is dangerous. Split push to scatter the enemy formation and target isolated squishy enemies, or flank the teamfight from the side/rear to assassinate the enemy carry, strictly following an assassin's movement of quickly disengaging using skills.
hero_044.teamfight: In teamfights, use the AoE multi-hit attack and super armor (CC immunity and damage reduction) of Skill 3 to throw the enemy formation into chaos. Jump in right after key enemy skills are used, disrupt them with knockups while dealing damage, and use your mobility to leave the frontlines before being focused down.
hero_044.commonMistakes: Because Skill 2 has a unique mechanic of dashing in the opposite direction of the cast, mistakes of accidentally jumping right into the middle of the enemy team or failing to escape are frequent. Also, overestimating your mobility and forgetting your low durability, jumping in while enemies still have CC available, will lead to instant death.
hero_044.strengths: The game's top-tier overwhelming mobility provided by three movement skills (two charges on Skill 1, one on Skill 2) is the biggest strength. This mobility grants high-level farming speed, split-pushing ability, escape potential, and chasing power, making him a highly powerful hero in macro play that dominates the entire map.
hero_044.weaknesses: Base durability stats are extremely low, making him fragile and easily killed without being able to do anything if hit by CC. Also, because the controls are busy and require getting used to the unique backward dash of Skill 2, he has the weakness of being extremely dependent on the player's mechanical skill and map awareness.

hero_045.earlyGame: Safely manage the laning phase by utilizing the Passive shield and "Cold" effect (reduced movement and attack speed). Harass enemies and chip away their HP from a distance using the icicle from the 3rd enhanced Basic Attack and the AoE damage and vision grant from Skill 1. The AoE damage and slow triggered when your shield breaks also helps in self-defense.
hero_045.midGame: While the freeze effect of Skill 2 is powerful, it has a delay before activating, so the basic combo is to slow the enemy's movement speed with the Cold effect of Skill 1 before placing Skill 2. Ambushing from bushes, dealing bonus damage to frozen enemies, and securing kills with your team is a highly powerful tactic.
hero_045.lateGame: In the late game where teamfights frequently occur, the wide-area blizzard of Skill 3 dictates the course of battle. Hinder enemy advances or retreats and split their formation with continuous damage every 0.5 seconds and the Cold effect. Activating Skill 3 immediately refreshes your Passive shield, so it can also be used to enhance your survivability.
hero_045.teamfight: Deploy Skill 3 in densely packed enemy areas or around objectives (towers, dragons) to zone enemies by spreading slows and damage over a wide area. The ideal scenario is landing the freeze from Skill 2 on enemies whose movement is restricted, creating a situation where your team can attack one-sidedly.
hero_045.commonMistakes: Because Skill 2's freeze has a delayed activation, throwing it randomly without predicting the enemy's movement will result in misses. Furthermore, lacking mobility and escape skills, stepping too far forward or letting an assassin get close means you will be taken down quickly despite the shield. Careful positioning is strictly required.
hero_045.strengths: All skills feature powerful CC such as the Cold effect (reduced movement and attack speed) or freezing, giving her extremely high enemy control capabilities. Also, possessing a wide-area continuous damage skill makes her excellent at breaking formations in teamfights and zoning during objective fights.
hero_045.weaknesses: Lacking any movement skills (blinks), her mobility is low, making her fragile in close-combat situations. Additionally, her greatest weapon, the freeze from Skill 2, is easily dodged if seen, making it difficult to land independently. It requires either comboing with an ally's CC or possessing high predictive skills to read enemy movements.

hero_046.earlyGame: Possessing high damage from the early game, the combo of applying a slow and mark with the dagger from Skill 2, followed by stunning and healing with the clone attack of Skill 1, is powerful. With a Passive that increases Movement Speed when moving toward enemies, aggressively target isolated enemies to secure early kills and widen your lead.
hero_046.midGame: Gaining high burst damage from the long-range dash of Skill 3 dramatically improves your assassination capabilities. Target squishy backline heroes like mages and marksmen for surprise attacks. Ensure the mark explosion from Skill 2 (24% missing HP damage) triggers to guarantee the kill before disengaging.
hero_046.lateGame: You slightly fall off in the late game when enemies group up and acquire defensive items. Avoid head-on teamfights; instead, sneak up from outside the enemy's vision and focus exclusively on instantly killing isolated farming enemies or the carry at the back of a teamfight. You are required to take down the enemy's key player, even if it results in a trade.
hero_046.teamfight: Diving straight into the middle of a teamfight will get you melted instantly, so positioning yourself to flank from the side or rear is mandatory. Wait for your team to initiate and for the enemy's CC skills to be consumed, then use the Skill 2 -> Skill 3 -> Skill 1 combo to instantly assassinate a squishy carry and escape.
hero_046.commonMistakes: Missing the dagger from Skill 2 prevents both the stun and the percentage damage of the mark explosion, drastically increasing the chance of a failed assassination. Another classic mistake is forcing an engage into an enemy group in the late game, failing to secure a kill, and being immediately counter-killed. Playstyle must account for your low durability.
hero_046.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming single-target assassination capability from the early to mid game, thanks to the slow, stun, percentage damage, and healing of Skill 2 combined with the high physical damage of Skills 1 and 3. Simply approaching enemies increases your Movement Speed, giving you excellent chasing power to prevent fleeing targets from escaping.
hero_046.weaknesses: Having low defense and HP, you are easily killed if hit by CC. Although you have target missing HP percentage damage, your ability to break through tank/support-guarded formations or impact late-game teamfights is low. A clear weakness is that the longer the game drags on, the harder it is to exert influence.

hero_047.earlyGame: Because data explaining this hero's skills is not provided, it is impossible to analyze specific early-game tactics or strategies. Following general game theory, it is recommended to focus on killing minions to earn EXP and gold, and to lane with a safety-first approach while keeping an eye on enemy movements.
hero_047.midGame: Since the provided factual data contains no descriptions regarding skills or traits, mid-game movements cannot be evaluated. Generally speaking, in the mid game, cooperate with your team to secure vision, aim for destroying towers and taking objectives like dragons, and keep a macro perspective of the entire map to avoid being isolated.
hero_047.lateGame: Because the skill set information is missing, it is impossible to construct a role or playstyle for the late game. Since teamfights directly dictate the outcome in the late game, it is crucial to act in accordance with your team's formation and avoid careless deaths. Strictly adhere to the basic movements of your role and prioritize teamwork.
hero_047.teamfight: Without skill information, it is impossible to explain specific damage output methods, positioning, or how to utilize CC in teamfights. As a general combat basic, position yourself behind tanks, wait for powerful enemy skills to be used before safely engaging, and maintain flexible responses depending on the situation.
hero_047.commonMistakes: Because the unique mechanics of this hero are unknown, common mistakes when using skills cannot be pointed out. Mistakes common to any hero include invading the enemy jungle without checking the map and getting surrounded, or forcing a fight in disadvantageous situations. Careful judgment is required.
hero_047.strengths: Since there is absolutely no data describing the skill effects, Passive benefits, or stat characteristics, it is impossible to evaluate this hero's strengths or favorable situations. Players must check the skills in the actual game and understand for themselves what advantages the hero possesses, such as high mobility or high burst damage.
hero_047.weaknesses: Because there is no skill data in the provided file, clear weaknesses or bad matchups cannot be identified. Generally, each hero has weaknesses such as low durability, lack of mobility, or vulnerability to specific CC, so it is essential to understand and compensate for these flaws through actual gameplay.

hero_048.earlyGame: In the early game, accumulate energy with Basic Attacks and use the Passive's Rapid Flight (Movement Speed boost) and piercing enhanced Basic Attacks to harass from a safe distance. Apply marks while dealing damage and slows with the laurel leaves of Skill 1, keeping in mind advantageous positioning as a magic damage-focused marksman.
hero_048.midGame: Enhance your offensive and defensive mobility by fully utilizing the full energy recovery, Attack Speed increase, and slow immunity of Skill 2. Accumulate 6 or more laurel marks to unlock Skill 3, and use its auto-tracking magic damage against low HP enemies within range to gain the upper hand in skirmishes and gank pursuits.
hero_048.lateGame: Since your Passive Movement Speed increases in proportion to your Magic Attack, you gain extremely high mobility in the late game. The continuous barrage of Skill 3, unleashed with maximum marks, boasts tremendous wave clear and kill potential. Maximize the benefits of physical/magical penetration and lifesteal to burst out damage from outside the enemy's range.
hero_048.teamfight: Rely on the Rapid Flight of your Passive and the slow immunity of Skill 2 to strictly kite enemy assassins and fighters, keeping your distance. Chip away at their health with Basic Attacks and enhanced Basic Attacks from a safe position, and when enemy HP is low, cast Skill 3 to clean up kills all at once with its auto-tracking attacks.
hero_048.commonMistakes: If you rely too much on the Passive's Movement Speed boost and step too far forward, you will be easily killed upon receiving hard CC (stuns or knockups). Also, since Skill 3 targets the enemy with the lowest HP within range, poor timing and positioning might cause it to be absorbed by enemy tanks, failing to deal effective damage.
hero_048.strengths: She has the unique trait where her Basic Attacks deal magic damage and apply the higher value between her physical and magical penetration, as well as lifesteal. With the energy-consuming dash (Rapid Flight) and Skill 2's slow immunity, her greatest strength is possessing extremely high mobility and kiting ability despite being a marksman.
hero_048.weaknesses: Lacking instant burst damage, it takes time to dish out enough damage to overcome enemy healing or shields. Furthermore, as a trade-off for high mobility, her base durability is extremely low, leaving her fragile and prone to being instantly killed if caught by targeted hard CC or unavoidable AoE burst damage.
